title: Protocols
text: Protocol calculators are Shiny documents — they live in protocols/_shiny/ and are served individually, not as part of the notebook site. They cannot be included in a static website, so they are not browsable here.

title: Protocols
section: How to open a protocol calculator
text:
How to open a protocol calculator
Protocol calculators must be served from inside protocols/_shiny/ — running quarto serve from the repo root fails because the parent _quarto.yml defines a website project, which is incompatible with Shiny.
cd protocols/_shiny

quarto serve HiC_protocol.qmd      # in situ Hi-C
quarto serve CnR_protocol.qmd      # CUT&RUN
quarto serve ENS_2D_protocol.qmd   # 2D ENS differentiation

# When done: Ctrl+C, then cd ../.. to return to the repo root
Each opens in your browser. Configure your inputs in the sidebar, then File → Print → Save as PDF for your bench copy.

title: Protocols
text:
Adding a new protocol
Give Claude the source (PDF, .docx, or paste the methods section) and say “make a QMD for this protocol.” Output goes in protocols/_shiny/. Then add a row to the table above.

title: Protocols
text:
Why the separation?
Quarto website projects render everything to static HTML. Shiny documents require a live R process and can’t be static. They coexist in the same repo but are used differently:

Notebook site (quarto render from project root) — static, browsable, searchable
Protocol calculators (cd protocols/_shiny && quarto serve X.qmd) — interactive, run on demand

title: Lab notebook
text: This is a living record of wet lab experiments, protocols, and reading notes. It is not a computational notebook — that lives separately. Every experiment entry is one .qmd file per run, committed to git when complete.

title: Lab notebook
text:
How to use this notebook
Starting a new experiment:
# Copy the relevant template
cp experiments/_templates/HiC_entry_template.qmd \
   experiments/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)_HiC-[target]-[celltype].qmd

# Open in Positron and fill in the planning section
# Then each evening: fill in that day's section, save

# When the experiment is fully done:
git add experiments/YYYY-MM-DD_HiC-target-celltype.qmd
git commit -m "Hi-C [target] [celltype] — [one-line result summary]"
Printing a protocol:
quarto serve protocols/HiC_protocol.qmd
# Configure Shiny inputs in browser → Cmd+P → Save as PDF
Rebuilding the site:
quarto render
# Open _site/index.html in browser
